# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.
# Category: cicd_security

stages: 
 - test   # The stage where all tests run on your runner   
 - deploy      # Stage for preparing production dependencies (npm install, npm build etc.) before releasing them into a registry such as NPM or Bower. Then they are released in the deployment pipeline step below this one    
- release       # Deploying changes to Production  and also creating new Docker image/release    Here you create tags for these releases   on your repository (git tag)      Release notes will be generated based from commit messages as per requirement. Also, CI can notify about the deployed artifacts in this stage
 - preprod     # Stage where deployments to Pre-Prod environment are performed and testing is done before pushing production changes  Deployment tags include all merge request titles (as comments)   Includes manual step of verifying that everything passed tests. Then these deployment tasks finish, if no failure occurred in the previous stages then it will proceed for preprod deployments   
 - prod         # Finally stage where final releases are deployed to Production after thorough testing and quality assurance has been complete  Deployment tags include all merge request titles (as comments)   Also creates new Docker image/release at this point. Once tests have passed, the deployment will finish here on production server    

deploy_prod:    # Define a job that runs before starting any stage but after defining its dependences in .gitlab-ci folder  Includes steps for CI and Deployment   Also generates Docker images/release notes based from commit messages. It also includes the deployment tags into release note section of git lab build notification
